name: install
context: Install Playwright packages and browser binaries for tests, standalone scripts, CI, proxies, and coding-agent CLI use.
info: This skill covers Playwright installation for the three common surfaces: @playwright/test for application test suites, playwright for standalone browser automation scripts, and @playwright/cli for coding-agent controlled sessions. It includes browser binary installation, Linux dependencies through --with-deps, package-manager detection, proxy and internal artifact mirrors, browser cache placement, and skip-download workflows for locked-down environments. Agents use it before writing tests, taking screenshots, running trace debugging, or automating a page in CI.
triggers: playwright in ci cannot find browser
body:
# Playwright Install

Install has two parts: the npm package and the browser binaries. A project can have `@playwright/test` installed but still fail at runtime if `npx playwright install` has not downloaded Chromium, Firefox, or WebKit.

## Quick Commands

```bash
# Test runner with all supported browsers
nodes/playwright/scripts/install-playwright.sh test chromium firefox webkit

# Standalone automation library with Chromium only
nodes/playwright/scripts/install-playwright.sh library chromium

# Coding-agent CLI
nodes/playwright/scripts/install-playwright.sh agent-cli
```

Manual equivalents:

```bash
npm init playwright@latest
npm install -D @playwright/test
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit

npm install -D playwright
npx playwright install chromium

npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest
```

## Environment

```bash
export HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxy.internal:8080
export PLAYWRIGHT_DOWNLOAD_HOST=https://artifacts.example.com/playwright
export PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=.cache/ms-playwright
export PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_BROWSER_DOWNLOAD=1
```

Use `--with-deps` on Linux CI runners so OS packages required by browsers are installed alongside binaries. Use `PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_BROWSER_DOWNLOAD=1` only when browser binaries are managed by an image, cache, or artifact repository.

sections.title: Choose package
sections.body: Use `@playwright/test` for test suites because it includes the runner, fixtures, assertions, reports, projects, retries, and traces. Use `playwright` when writing plain Node scripts that launch a browser directly. Use `@playwright/cli` when a coding agent needs command-by-command browser control and page snapshots. Do not install all three by default; choose the surface the task needs.

sections.title: Browser binaries
sections.body: Playwright downloads patched browser builds separately from npm packages. Run `npx playwright install` after dependency install, or specify a subset such as `npx playwright install chromium`. In CI and containers, prefer `npx playwright install --with-deps` so Linux system dependencies are present. Cache `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH` when CI cache policy allows it.

sections.title: Restricted networks
sections.body: For proxies, set `HTTPS_PROXY` before `npx playwright install`. For internal mirrors, set `PLAYWRIGHT_DOWNLOAD_HOST` to the artifact host. For pre-baked Docker images, set `PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_BROWSER_DOWNLOAD=1` during package install and ensure `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH` points at the installed binaries. If downloads fail, verify the package version and browser revision match.

sections.title: Verify installation
sections.body: Run `npx playwright --version`, then `npx playwright test --list` for test projects or a minimal `node -e "import('playwright').then(async ({ chromium }) => { const b = await chromium.launch(); await b.close(); })"` for library projects. Browser launch failures usually mean missing binaries, missing Linux dependencies, a sandbox constraint, or a package/browser revision mismatch.

related.note: Playwright packages are installed through Node package managers.
CI runners need browser binaries and OS dependencies.
Docker images often preinstall browser dependencies and cache browser binaries.

scripts.name: Install Playwright
scripts.description: Install @playwright/test, playwright, or @playwright/cli and optionally install browser binaries with --with-deps.

PLAYWRIGHT_PACKAGE_MANAGER.description: Override package manager: npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun.

PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_PACKAGE.description: Skip npm package installation and only install browser binaries.

HTTPS_PROXY.description: Proxy used when downloading browser binaries.

PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH.description: Custom path for browser binaries and CI caches.

PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_BROWSER_DOWNLOAD.description: Skip browser download during package installation.

withDeps.description: Install Linux system dependencies with browser binaries.

depends.reason: Playwright package installation and npx commands require Node package tooling.

author: dojo-community
